notes: Real-repository smoke corpus (internal/corpus). Six repos cover the historical first-contact bug classes (non-source assets, symlinked layouts, JSON fixtures) plus language spread: Go (also the future typeresolve proving ground), Python, Ruby, TypeScript, JavaScript+JSON, and a Java/JVM Maven monorepo (guava, tier 3 — nightly/manual, excluded from the PR gate via -max-tier 2). Refs pin RELEASE TAGS; sha additionally pins the checkout HEAD (case-insensitive prefix, >=12 hex chars — recorded from the first green CI run, 2026-07-02; guava recorded via git ls-remote refs/tags/v33.0.0 on 2026-07-14, a lightweight tag whose object IS the commit). If an upstream tag is ever re-pointed, the pin step fails closed. Adding a repository is a data change here, never a code change.

entries.name: cobra
entries.notes: Go CLI framework; the typeresolve (v0.2.0) acceptance target. The confirmed_edges assertion is the acceptance gate: Command.Execute calls c.ExecuteC() — a receiver-method dispatch the name heuristic cannot prove — and the go/types pass must carry it at the confirmed tier.

entries.name: flask
entries.notes: Python package; docs, JSON, and mixed non-source assets.

entries.name: express
entries.notes: JavaScript + many JSON fixtures and markdown — the malformed-asset bug class.

entries.name: guava
entries.notes: Java/JVM Maven monorepo (guava, guava-testlib, guava-gwt, android flavor) — the master-plan EVAL-01 monorepo requirement. Tier 3: large repo, runs nightly/manual and in the EVAL-02 workflow, never in the PR gate.

entries.name: tier1-fixture-go
entries.notes: Tier-1 pinned local fixture for the PR-gate path. No network required.

entries.name: tier1-fixture-hero-go
entries.notes: Tier-1 pinned multi-file Go fixture for the SW-122 hero tasks (cross-file callers, related_files ranking). The single-file go fixture is frozen as the SW-110 byte-parity oracle and must not grow, so multi-file shapes live here. No network required.
